name: Cocoa Cake
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup (1 1/2 sticks) margarine at room temperature
Liquid sugar substitute equal to 1/2 cup sugar
3 large egg whites, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together cocoa and boiling water to blend. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
2. Cream margarine at medium speed until light and fluffy.
3. Add sweetener, vanilla, cooled cocoa mixture, and liquid sugar substitute to the creamed mixture. Mix until well blended.
4. Beat egg whites at medium speed until foamy. Gradually add cream of tartar and sugar, beating at high speed to form a meringue. Set aside for later use.
5. Stir together flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon. Add 1 cup cool water to the creamed mixture along with the dry ingredients. Beat until well blended.
6. Carefully stir batter into greased pans lined with wax paper on the bottom.
7. Bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes or until a cake tester comes out clean from the center and the cake pulls away from the sides of the pan.
8. Turn cake onto a cooling rack and allow to cool to room temperature. Spread with diabetic jelly between layers and frost with fluffy frosting.
9. Cut into 16 equal servings.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 1/2 BREAD EXCHANGE + 2 FAT EXCHANGE; CAL: 170; CHO: 20gm; PRO: 3gm; FAT: 9gm
LOW-SODIUM DIET: Omit salt. Use salt-free margarine and low-sodium baking powder.
